name: Discord-C2-Framework
summary: This project is an educational initiative designed to demonstrate the risks and vulnerabilities of using trusted platforms, like Discord, for malicious purposes. It highlights how attackers can exploit Discord as a Command-and-Control (C2) channel to remotely control compromised Windows systems. The purpose of this project is to educate and raise awareness about cybersecurity threats.

documentation.readme: This project is an educational initiative designed to demonstrate the risks and vulnerabilities of using trusted platforms, like Discord, for malicious purposes. It highlights how attackers can exploit Discord as a Command-and-Control (C2) channel to remotely control compromised Windows systems. The purpose of this project is to educate and raise awareness about cybersecurity threats.
usage_sections: Leveraging Trusted Platforms: Demonstrates how Discord’s infrastructure can be exploited for malicious activities while mimicking normal traffic.
SSL-Enforced Encryption: Ensures all communication between the attacker and the target is encrypted, making it challenging for security measures to detect malicious activities.
Practical Demonstration: Showcases step-by-step instructions for bot setup, payload deployment, and executing commands on the target system.
Capabilities of Exploitation: Commands demonstrated include:
Capturing screenshots from the compromised system.

function_signatures.description: Index entry in the Command and Control category. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner after a NVD/lookup pass on the target vendor.

function_signatures.name: pairs_with_the_command_and_control_system_prompt_stanza_in_core_ai_backend_chain_to_teach_the_llm_the_standard_invocation_pattern
function_signatures.description: Pairs with the Command and Control system-prompt stanza in core.ai_backend.chain to teach the LLM the standard invocation pattern.

function_signatures.name: operator_initiated_the_chain_step_is_gated_by_the_per_step_accept_cancel_gate_the_tool_does_not_auto_execute
function_signatures.description: Operator-initiated: the chain step is gated by the per-step ACCEPT/CANCEL gate; the tool does not auto-execute.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for Discord-C2-Framework (ajoyag/Discord-C2-Framework). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for Discord-C2-Framework. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Command and Control, use Discord-C2-Framework because This project is an educational initiative designed to demonstrate the risks and vulnerabilities of using trusted platforms, like Discord, for malicious purposes.
When the engagement is in the post_exploit phase, use Discord-C2-Framework as a cataloged Command and Control tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Command and Control tools, pair Discord-C2-Framework with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Discord-C2-Framework --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
